name: Lentils and Bulgar Pilaf
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound lentils, washed and picked over
2 garlic cloves, minced or put through a press
2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley or cilantro
1/2 cup plain low-fat yogurt for topping
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the lentils in water in a large casserole for 1 hour.
2. Add garlic, bay leaf, and cumin to the water. Bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat and simmer 20-30 minutes until lentils are tender.
4. Toward the end of cooking, add cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper to taste.
5. Stir bulgur into the lentils (ensure plenty of liquid remains). Cover and turn off heat.
6. Let sit for 20-30 minutes until bulgur absorbs liquid from lentils and becomes tender.
7. Meanwhile, saute onions in olive oil over low heat in a nonstick skillet until almost caramelized.
8. Taste bulgur and lentils; adjust seasonings as needed.
9. Transfer mixture to serving platter and sprinkle with onions and parsley or cilantro.
10. Serve topped with plain low-fat yogurt, with crisp green salad on the side.
Makes: 6-8 servings
